informal_statement: Let M be a finitely presented module over a commutative ring R, let N be an R-module, and let S be a multiplicative subset of R. Any surjective linear map between the localizations of M and N at S lifts to a linear map M → N that becomes surjective after localizing at S.
gold_formal:
lemma Module.exists_localizedMap_surjective_of_surjective [Module.FinitePresentation R M]
    (S : Submonoid R) {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ]
    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] {Nₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Nₚ] [Module R Nₚ]
    (g : N →ₗ[R] Nₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S g] {ϕ : Mₚ →ₗ[R] Nₚ} (hϕ : Function.Surjective ϕ) :
    ∃ (φ : M →ₗ[R] N) (s : S) (_ : IsLocalizedModule.map S f g φ = s • ϕ),
      Function.Surjective (IsLocalizedModule.map S f g φ) := sorry
gold_context: variable {R M N N'} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
variable [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] (S : Submonoid R) (f : N →ₗ[R] N') [IsLocalizedModule S f]
determinacy_reason: lifting a surjection of localizations; hypotheses (finitely presented, localization) expressible informally.

informal_statement: Two additive group homomorphisms out of a monoid algebra R[M] that agree on every single-monomial element (the basis element supported at m with coefficient r) are equal.
gold_formal:
lemma addMonoidHom_ext [AddZeroClass N] ⦃f g : R[M] →+ N⦄
    (h : ∀ m r, f (single m r) = g (single m r)) : f = g := sorry
gold_context: variable {R S G M N O ι : Type*}
variable [Semiring R] {x y : R[M]} {r r₁ r₂ : R} {m m' m₁ m₂ m₁' m₂' : M}
determinacy_reason: extensionality on monomials; hypothesis and conclusion unambiguous.

informal_statement: In a monoid algebra, for a fixed nonzero coefficient r, the map sending a monoid element m to the monomial r·m (the single element supported at m with coefficient r) is injective.
gold_formal: lemma single_left_injective (hr : r ≠ 0) : Function.Injective fun m : M ↦ single m r := sorry
gold_context: variable {R S G M N O ι : Type*}
variable [Semiring R] {x y : R[M]} {r r₁ r₂ : R} {m m' m₁ m₂ m₁' m₂' : M}
determinacy_reason: injectivity of the monomial map for nonzero coefficient; determinate.

informal_statement: Let f and g be elements of a monoid algebra over a ring, and let a0, b0 be monoid elements such that a0·b0 can be written as a product of an element of the support of f and an element of the support of g in exactly one way. Then the coefficient of a0·b0 in the product f·g equals the product of the coefficient of a0 in f and the coefficient of b0 in g.
determinacy_reason: the unique-product hypothesis and the coefficient formula are expressible informally.

informal_statement: Every element of a group algebra k[G] lies in the k-submodule spanned by the group-element basis vectors indexed by its own support.
gold_formal:
theorem mem_span_support_coeff [MulOneClass G] (f : k[G]) :
    f ∈ Submodule.span k (of k G '' f.coeff.support) := sorry
gold_context: variable {k : Type u₁} {G : Type u₂} [Semiring k]
determinacy_reason: clear membership statement about span of support; determinate.

informal_statement: Scalar multiplication on the left by a fixed non-negative element preserves antitonicity: if f is non-increasing then x ↦ a·f(x) is non-increasing for a ≥ 0.
gold_formal:
lemma Antitone.const_smul [PosSMulMono α β] {γ : Type*} [Preorder γ] {f : γ → β}
    (hf : Antitone f) (ha : 0 ≤ a) : Antitone fun x ↦ a • f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {α β} {a a₁ a₂ : α} {b b₁ b₂ : β}
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the ordered-smul family (monotone/antitone × left/right × non-strict/strict); the informal wording matches several sibling lemmas.

informal_statement: Scalar multiplication on the left by a fixed non-negative element preserves monotonicity: if f is monotone then x ↦ a·f(x) is monotone for a ≥ 0.
gold_formal:
lemma Monotone.const_smul [PosSMulMono α β] {γ : Type*} [Preorder γ] {f : γ → β}
    (hf : Monotone f) (ha : 0 ≤ a) : Monotone fun x ↦ a • f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {α β} {a a₁ a₂ : α} {b b₁ b₂ : β}
determinacy_reason: determinate; representative of the ordered-smul family.

informal_statement: Scalar multiplication on the left by a fixed positive element preserves strict monotonicity: if f is strictly increasing then x ↦ a·f(x) is strictly increasing for a > 0.
gold_formal:
lemma StrictMono.const_smul [PosSMulStrictMono α β] {γ : Type*} [Preorder γ] {f : γ → β}
    (hf : StrictMono f) (ha : 0 < a) : StrictMono fun x ↦ a • f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {α β} {a a₁ a₂ : α} {b b₁ b₂ : β}
determinacy_reason: determinate; strict/positive representative.

informal_statement: For a polynomial f with coefficients in a ring, the support of the Laurent polynomial obtained from f (its set of nonzero-coefficient exponents) is the image, under the inclusion of the naturals into the integers, of the support of f.
gold_formal:
theorem support_coeff_toLaurent (f : R[X]) :
    f.toLaurent.coeff.support = f.support.map Nat.castEmbedding := sorry
determinacy_reason: the support-equals-image-of-support statement is unambiguous.

informal_statement: Let F be a field and v a finite family of nontrivial, pairwise inequivalent real-valued absolute values on F. Then the diagonal embedding of F into the product of the copies of F, each carrying the topology of its absolute value v_i, has dense image. (Abstract weak approximation / Artin–Whaples.)
gold_formal:
theorem denseRange_algebraMap_pi {ι : Type*} [Finite ι] {v : ι → AbsoluteValue F ℝ}
    (h : ∀ i, (v i).IsNontrivial)
    (hv : Pairwise fun i j ↦ ¬(v i).IsEquiv (v j)) :
    DenseRange <| algebraMap F ((i : ι) → WithAbs (v i)) := sorry
determinacy_reason: named theorem (weak approximation); the finiteness, nontriviality, pairwise-inequivalence hypotheses and the dense-image conclusion are all clear informally.

informal_statement: The norm-determinant (the absolute value of the determinant, defined via orthonormal bases) of a linear isometry between inner product spaces is 1.
gold_formal: theorem _root_.LinearIsometry.normDet_eq_one (f : U →ₗᵢ[𝕜] V) : f.toLinearMap.normDet = 1 := sorry
gold_context: variable {𝕜 U V W : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup U] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 U]
determinacy_reason: clean value statement; determinate.

informal_statement: For an endomorphism of a finite-dimensional inner product space, its norm-determinant equals the norm (absolute value) of its ordinary determinant.
gold_formal: theorem normDet_eq_norm_det (f : U →ₗ[𝕜] U) : f.normDet = ‖f.det‖ := sorry
gold_context: variable {𝕜 U V W : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup U] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 U]
determinacy_reason: normDet = |det| for endomorphisms; determinate.

informal_statement: The square of the norm-determinant of a linear map equals the determinant of the Gram matrix formed from the images of an orthonormal basis under the map.
gold_formal:
theorem normDet_sq_eq_det_gram {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (f : U →ₗ[𝕜] V)
    (b : OrthonormalBasis ι 𝕜 U) :
    ↑(f.normDet ^ 2) = (Matrix.gram 𝕜 (f <| b ·)).det := sorry
gold_context: variable {𝕜 U V W : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup U] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 U]
determinacy_reason: normDet² = det of Gram matrix; determinate.

informal_statement: In a two-dimensional inner product space, if a nonzero vector w and a vector u are both orthogonal to the same nonzero vector v, then u lies in the line spanned by w.
gold_formal:
theorem mem_span_singleton_of_inner_eq_zero_of_inner_eq_zero
    [Fact (finrank 𝕜 E = 2)] {u v w : E} (hv : v ≠ 0) (hw : w ≠ 0)
    (huv : ⟪v, u⟫_𝕜 = 0) (hwv : ⟪v, w⟫_𝕜 = 0) :
    u ∈ 𝕜 ∙ w := sorry
gold_context: open Topology Finsupp Submodule RCLike Real Filter InnerProductSpace
determinacy_reason: the 2-dimensional orthogonality/parallelism fact; determinate.

informal_statement: A meromorphic function has non-negative order at a point if and only if it agrees, near that point (off the point itself), with some function that is analytic there. In other words, non-negative order is equivalent to the existence of an analytic extension across the point.
gold_formal:
theorem MeromorphicAt.meromorphicOrderAt_nonneg_iff
    (hf : MeromorphicAt f x) :
    0 ≤ meromorphicOrderAt f x ↔ ∃ g : 𝕜 → E, AnalyticAt 𝕜 g x ∧ f =ᶠ[𝓝[≠] x] g := sorry
determinacy_reason: order-nonneg ⇔ analytic extension; expressible informally.

informal_statement: For real numbers a, b both greater than 1, the integral from a to b of 1/(t·log t) equals log(log b) − log(log a).
gold_formal:
theorem integral_inv_div_log (ha : 1 < a) (hb : 1 < b) :
    ∫ t in a..b, t⁻¹ / log t = log (log b) - log (log a) := sorry
determinacy_reason: explicit definite integral; determinate.

informal_statement: For real numbers a, b both greater than 1, the integral from a to b of 1/(t·(log t)²) equals 1/log a − 1/log b.
gold_formal:
theorem integral_inv_div_log_sq (ha : 1 < a) (hb : 1 < b) :
    ∫ t in a..b, t⁻¹ / log t ^ 2 = (log a)⁻¹ - (log b)⁻¹ := sorry
determinacy_reason: explicit definite integral; determinate.

informal_statement: For every real number x, the absolute value of x minus the sine of x is at most the absolute value of x cubed divided by six.
gold_formal: theorem abs_sub_sin_le (x : ℝ) : |x - Real.sin x| ≤ |x| ^ 3 / 6 := sorry
determinacy_reason: explicit inequality |x - sin x| ≤ |x|³/6; fully determinate.

informal_statement: For every non-negative real number x, x minus x cubed over six is at most the sine of x.
gold_formal: theorem sin_ge_sub_cube {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) : x - x ^ 3 / 6 ≤ Real.sin x := sorry
determinacy_reason: explicit inequality; determinate.

informal_statement: Let f be non-negative, antitone (non-increasing) and integrable on the ray (N-1, ∞) for a positive integer N. Then the absolute difference between the infinite sum Σ f(n) over all naturals and the partial sum of the first N terms is at most the integral of f over (N-1, ∞).
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.abs_tsum_sub_sum_range_le_integral {N : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N)
    (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici (N - 1 : ℝ)))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi (N - 1 : ℝ))) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi (N - 1 : ℝ), 0 ≤ f t) :
    |(∑' (n : ℕ), f n) - ∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, f n| ≤ ∫ x in Ioi (N - 1 : ℝ), f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: the tail-vs-integral bound is expressible; hypotheses explicit.

informal_statement: A non-negative, integrable, summable, antitone function is integrable on a ray (converse to the integral test).
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.integrableOn_Ioi_of_summable_comp_add {N : ℕ} (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici (N : ℝ)))
    (summable : Summable (fun n ↦ f (n + N : ℕ))) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi (N : ℝ), 0 ≤ f t) :
    IntegrableOn f (Ioi (N : ℝ)) := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the summable-converse lemma stated over (0,∞); the informal wording underdetermines the base point.

informal_statement: For a non-negative antitone summable function f on [0,∞), the integral of f over (0,∞) is at most the infinite sum Σ f(n) over the naturals.
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.integral_le_tsum (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici 0))
    (summable : Summable (fun (n : ℕ) ↦ f n)) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi 0, 0 ≤ f t) :
    ∫ x in Ioi 0, f x ≤ ∑' (n : ℕ),  f n := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: integral ≤ sum bound; determinate.

informal_statement: The partial sums of a non-negative antitone function are bounded by its integral over a ray.
gold_formal:
lemma AntitoneOn.sum_Ico_le_integral {a b : ℕ} (anti : AntitoneOn f (Icc a b))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi a)) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi (a : ℝ), 0 ≤ f t) :
    ∑ n ∈ .Ico a b, f ↑(n + 1) ≤ ∫ x in Ioi (a : ℝ), f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate: informal does not fix the endpoints (Ico a b vs range N) that distinguish the sibling lemmas.

informal_statement: For a non-negative function f that is antitone and integrable on [0,∞), the partial sum f(1)+…+f(N) is at most the integral of f over (0,∞).
gold_formal:
lemma AntitoneOn.sum_range_le_integral {N : ℕ} (anti : AntitoneOn f (Icc 0 (N : ℝ)))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi 0)) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi 0, 0 ≤ f t) :
    ∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, f ((n + 1 : ℕ)) ≤ ∫ x in Ioi 0, f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: partial-sum ≤ integral bound; determinate.

informal_statement: Integral test: a function that is non-negative, integrable, and antitone (non-increasing) on a ray [N,∞) is summable over the naturals.
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.summable_of_integrableOn_Ioi {N : ℕ} (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici (N : ℝ)))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi (N : ℝ))) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi (N : ℝ), 0 ≤ f t) :
    Summable (fun (n : ℕ) ↦ f n) := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: the integral test; hypotheses and summability conclusion clear.

informal_statement: Integral test: a non-negative antitone function that is integrable on [0,∞) is summable.
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.summable_of_integrableOn_Ioi_zero (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici 0))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi 0)) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi 0, 0 ≤ f t) :
    Summable (fun (n : ℕ) ↦ f n) := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the general integral test (item over [N,∞)); the informal statement does not pin the base point 0 vs N, so several distinct Lean lemmas match.

informal_statement: The sum from index 1 of a non-negative antitone integrable function is bounded by its integral over (0,∞).
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.tsum_add_one_le_integral (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici 0))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi 0)) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi 0, 0 ≤ f t) :
    ∑' (n : ℕ),  f (n + 1 : ℕ) ≤ ∫ x in Ioi 0, f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the tsum-vs-integral bounds; the index shift is not informally distinguishable.

informal_statement: For a non-negative antitone integrable function f on [0,∞), the infinite sum Σ f(n) is at most f(0) plus the integral of f over (0,∞).
gold_formal:
theorem AntitoneOn.tsum_le_integral (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici 0))
    (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi 0)) (nonneg : ∀ t ∈ Ioi 0, 0 ≤ f t) :
    ∑' (n : ℕ),  f n ≤ f 0 + ∫ x in Ioi 0, f x := sorry
gold_context: variable {x₀ : ℝ} {a b : ℕ} {f g : ℝ → ℝ}
determinacy_reason: sum ≤ f(0) + integral; determinate and distinct from the other bounds.

informal_statement: Consider a commutative-cube diagram: if five of its six square faces commute and one of the edges into the target of the sixth face is an epimorphism, then the sixth face also commutes.
gold_formal:
theorem cube_lemma_of_epi (h0xx : f0x0 ≫ f01x = f00x ≫ f0x1) (h1xx : f1x0 ≫ f11x = f10x ≫ f1x1)
    (hx0x : fx00 ≫ f10x = f00x ≫ fx01) (hx1x : fx10 ≫ f11x = f01x ≫ fx11)
    (hxx0 : f0x0 ≫ fx10 = fx00 ≫ f1x0) [Epi f00x] : f0x1 ≫ fx11 = fx01 ≫ f1x1 := sorry
determinacy_reason: the cube lemma; 'five faces commute + one edge epi ⇒ sixth commutes' is expressible informally.

informal_statement: Consider a commutative-cube diagram: if five of its six square faces commute and one of the edges out of the source of the sixth face is a monomorphism, then the sixth face also commutes.
gold_formal:
theorem cube_lemma_of_mono (h0xx : f0x0 ≫ f01x = f00x ≫ f0x1) (h1xx : f1x0 ≫ f11x = f10x ≫ f1x1)
    (hx0x : fx00 ≫ f10x = f00x ≫ fx01) (hx1x : fx10 ≫ f11x = f01x ≫ fx11)
    (hxx1 : f0x1 ≫ fx11 = fx01 ≫ f1x1) [Mono f11x] : f0x0 ≫ fx10 = fx00 ≫ f1x0 := sorry
determinacy_reason: dual cube lemma (mono version); expressible informally but very close to the epi version.

informal_statement: Pentagonal number theorem (product form): as a formal power series, the infinite product over n ≥ 1 of (1 − X^n) converges to the series whose coefficients are supported on the generalized pentagonal numbers with alternating signs.
gold_formal:
theorem hasProd_one_sub_X_pow :
    HasProd (fun n ↦ (1 - X ^ (n + 1) : R⟦X⟧)) (pentagonalSeries R) := sorry
determinacy_reason: Euler's pentagonal number theorem as an infinite product; named theorem, expressible informally.

informal_statement: Pentagonal number theorem (sum form): the formal power series expressing the pentagonal-number series equals the sum over all integers k of (−1)^k X^{g(k)}, where g(k) = k(3k−1)/2 is the k-th generalized pentagonal number.
gold_formal:
theorem hasSum_pentagonalSeries :
    HasSum (fun k : ℤ ↦ (Int.negOnePow k : R⟦X⟧) * X ^ pentagonal k) (pentagonalSeries R) := sorry
determinacy_reason: the explicit alternating sum over generalized pentagonal exponents; determinate.

informal_statement: If two boolean predicates agree on every element of a list, then the first element the list finds satisfying one predicate is the same as for the other.
gold_formal:
theorem find?_congr {p₁ p₂ : α → Bool} {l : List α} (h : ∀ x ∈ l, p₁ x = p₂ x) :
    l.find? p₁ = l.find? p₂ := sorry
determinacy_reason: find? congruence under agreeing predicates; determinate.

informal_statement: If at most one element of a list satisfies a predicate, then searching for the first element satisfying that predicate gives the same result on any permutation of the list.
determinacy_reason: permutation-invariance of find? under a subsingleton hypothesis; determinate.

informal_statement: A map f between C^n manifolds is C^n if and only if it is continuous and its composition with a C^n immersion into a further manifold is C^n.
gold_formal:
lemma _root_.ContMDiff.iff_comp_isImmersion {f : M → N} {φ : N → N'} (hφ : IsImmersion J J' n φ) :
    CMDiff n f ↔ Continuous f ∧ CMDiff n (φ ∘ f) := sorry
determinacy_reason: C^n ⇔ continuous ∧ (immersion ∘ f) is C^n; expressible informally.

informal_statement: A map f between C^n manifolds is C^n at a point if and only if it is continuous at that point and its composition with a C^n immersion is C^n at that point.
gold_formal:
lemma _root_.ContMDiffAt.iff_comp_isImmersionAt {f : M → N} {φ : N → N'}
    (hφ : IsImmersionAt J J' n φ (f x)) :
    -- Note: `φ` need not be inducing, so continuity of `φ ∘ f` at `x`
    -- generally does not imply continuity of `f`
    CMDiffAt n f x ↔ ContinuousAt f x ∧ CMDiffAt n (φ ∘ f) x := sorry
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate: the pointwise 'at x' version is not informally distinguishable from the global one without naming the point.

informal_statement: A map f between C^n manifolds is C^n if and only if it is continuous and its composition with a C^n immersion into a further manifold is C^n.
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the immersion characterization; the informal does not distinguish the ordinary-immersion from the immersion-of-complement variant.

informal_statement: A C^n immersion between manifolds is itself C^n (smooth of the same class).
gold_formal: theorem contMDiff (h : IsImmersionOfComplement F I J n f) : CMDiff n f := sorry
determinacy_reason: concept is clear but 'immersion' informally underdetermines which bespoke immersion notion is meant; excluded from primary analysis.

informal_statement: Let G be a commutative group and h a real-valued function on G with finite sublevel sets (for every bound B only finitely many elements x satisfy h(x) ≤ B). If there is a natural number n and reals b > 1, c such that h(xⁿ) ≥ b·h(x) − c for all x, then the torsion subgroup of G is finite.
gold_header:
import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Archimedean.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Order
import Mathlib.Data.Set.Finite.Lemmas
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp
import Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith
determinacy_reason: descent/Northcott finiteness; all hypotheses (finite sublevel sets, growth inequality) expressible informally.

informal_statement: Let G be a commutative group and h a real-valued function on G with finite sublevel sets. If there is a constant C with |h(xy) + h(x/y) − 2h(x) − 2h(y)| ≤ C for all x, y (an approximate parallelogram law), then the torsion subgroup of G is finite.
gold_header:
import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Archimedean.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Order
import Mathlib.Data.Set.Finite.Lemmas
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp
import Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith
determinacy_reason: quasi-quadratic height ⇒ finite torsion; distinct hypothesis from the multiplicative-growth version.

informal_statement: A localization of a unique factorization monoid is again a unique factorization monoid.
gold_formal:
theorem UniqueFactorizationMonoid.of_isLocalization (N : Type*) [CommSemiring N] [Algebra M N]
    [IsLocalization S N] [UniqueFactorizationMonoid M] : UniqueFactorizationMonoid N := sorry
determinacy_reason: clean closure property; determinate.

informal_statement: Let R ≤ S ≤ N be a tower where N is a module over both R and S compatibly and S is the fraction field of R. Then the S-dimension of N equals its R-dimension.
gold_formal: theorem IsFractionRing.finrank_right_eq : finrank S N = finrank R N := sorry
gold_context: universe uR uS uT uM uN uP
variable {R : Type uR} (S : Type uS) {M : Type uM} {N : Type uN}
variable [Module R M] [Module R N] [Algebra R S] [Module S N] [IsScalarTower R S N]
variable (p : Submonoid R) [IsLocalization p S] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule p f]
determinacy_reason: finrank over the fraction ring equals finrank over the base; context supplies the tower/fraction-ring hypotheses.

informal_statement: For a tower where the middle ring is the fraction field of the base, the rank of the top module over the middle ring equals its rank over the base.
gold_formal: theorem IsFractionRing.rank_right_eq : Module.rank S N = Module.rank R N := sorry
gold_context: universe uR uS uT uM uN uP
variable {R : Type uR} (S : Type uS) {M : Type uM} {N : Type uN}
variable [Module R M] [Module R N] [Algebra R S] [Module S N] [IsScalarTower R S N]
variable (p : Submonoid R) [IsLocalization p S] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule p f]
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the finrank version; the informal 'dimension/rank' does not distinguish finrank from Module.rank.

informal_statement: A torsion module over a ring (with the module nonzero-divisor structure) has rank zero.
gold_formal:
theorem Module.IsTorsion.rank_eq_zero {R M : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
    [Nontrivial R] (h : IsTorsion R M) : Module.rank R M = 0 := sorry
determinacy_reason: torsion module has rank 0; determinate.

informal_statement: A finitely-semisimple endomorphism is triangularizable if and only if it is diagonalizable; equivalently, its generalized eigenspaces span the whole space if and only if its ordinary eigenspaces do.
gold_formal:
lemma IsFinitelySemisimple.iSup_maxGenEigenspace_eq_top_iff (hf : f.IsFinitelySemisimple) :
    (⨆ μ : R, f.maxGenEigenspace μ) = ⊤ ↔ (⨆ μ : R, f.eigenspace μ) = ⊤ := sorry
gold_context: variable {R M : Type*} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {f g : End R M}
determinacy_reason: triangularizable ⇔ diagonalizable for finitely-semisimple; expressible informally.

informal_statement: Every square matrix over a field can be transformed, by multiplying on the left and right by invertible matrices, into a block-diagonal form consisting of an identity block whose size is the rank of the matrix followed by a zero block.

    ∃ (V U : Matrix m m R) (e : m ≃ Fin M.rank ⊕ Fin (Fintype.card m - M.rank)),
      IsUnit V ∧ IsUnit U ∧
      V * M * U = (fromBlocks 1 0 0 0).submatrix e e := sorry

informal_statement: Let R be a commutative Noetherian ring and I an ideal of R. Let M be a finitely generated R-module with I·M a proper submodule of M, and N a finitely generated R-module whose support equals the zero locus (vanishing set) of I. Fix a natural number n. If the Ext groups Ext^i(N, M) vanish for every i < n, then there exists an M-regular sequence of length n all of whose entries lie in I.
gold_formal:
lemma exists_isRegular_of_exists_subsingleton_ext [Small.{v} R] [IsNoetherianRing R] (I : Ideal R)
    (n : ℕ) (M : ModuleCat.{v} R) [Module.Finite R M] (smul_lt : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) < ⊤)
    (N : ModuleCat.{v} R) [Module.Finite R N]
    (h_supp : Module.support R N = PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus I)
    (h_ext : ∀ i < n, Subsingleton (Ext N M i)) :
    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧ IsRegular M rs := sorry
gold_context: open LinearMap RingTheory.Sequence Ideal CategoryTheory Abelian Limits Pointwise IsSMulRegular
determinacy_reason: hypotheses (Noetherian, f.g., support = V(I), Ext vanishing) and conclusion (existence of a regular sequence in I of given length) are all expressible informally.

informal_statement: Let R be a commutative Noetherian ring, I an ideal, and M a nontrivial finitely generated R-module with I·M a proper submodule of M; fix a natural number n. The following are equivalent: (1) for every nontrivial finitely generated R-module N whose support is contained in the zero locus of I, the Ext groups Ext^i(N, M) vanish for all i < n; (2) the Ext groups Ext^i(R/I, M) vanish for all i < n; (3) there exists a nontrivial finitely generated R-module N whose support equals the zero locus of I with Ext^i(N, M) vanishing for all i < n; (4) there exists an M-regular sequence of length n with every entry in I.
gold_formal:
lemma exists_isRegular_tfae [Small.{v} R] [IsNoetherianRing R] (I : Ideal R) (n : ℕ)
    (M : ModuleCat.{v} R) [Module.Finite R M] (smul_lt : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) < ⊤) :
    [∀ N : ModuleCat.{v} R, Nontrivial N → Module.Finite R N →
      Module.support R N ⊆ PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus I → ∀ i < n, Subsingleton (Ext N M i),
      ∀ i < n, Subsingleton (Ext (ModuleCat.of R (Shrink.{v} (R ⧸ I))) M i),
      ∃ N : ModuleCat R, Nontrivial N ∧ Module.Finite R N ∧
      Module.support R N = PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus I ∧ ∀ i < n, Subsingleton (Ext N M i),
      ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs
      ].TFAE := sorry
gold_context: open LinearMap RingTheory.Sequence Ideal CategoryTheory Abelian Limits Pointwise IsSMulRegular
determinacy_reason: the four equivalent conditions are each expressible informally; this is the classical Rees theorem characterizing depth.

informal_statement: A function valued in a metric space has bounded variation on any finite subset of its domain.
gold_formal:
theorem _root_.BoundedVariationOn.of_finite {E} [PseudoMetricSpace E] (f : α → E) (s : Set α)
[Finite s] : BoundedVariationOn f s := sorry
gold_context: open scoped NNReal ENNReal Topology UniformConvergence
determinacy_reason: bounded variation on a finite set; determinate.

informal_statement: For a monotone sequence of points, the total variation of a function along the first n+1 of them equals the sum of the distances between the images of consecutive points.
gold_formal:
theorem image_range_of_monotone (f : α → E) {u : ℕ → α} (hu : Monotone u) (n : ℕ) :
    eVariationOn f (u '' Iic n) = ∑ i ∈ .range n, edist (f (u i)) (f (u (i + 1))) := sorry
gold_context: open scoped NNReal ENNReal Topology UniformConvergence
determinacy_reason: variation along a monotone finite chain is the sum of consecutive gaps; determinate.

informal_statement: The total variation of a function on a two-point set {a, b} equals the distance between the two function values f(a) and f(b).
gold_formal: theorem pair (f : α → E) (a b : α) : eVariationOn f {a, b} = edist (f a) (f b) := sorry
gold_context: open scoped NNReal ENNReal Topology UniformConvergence
determinacy_reason: variation on a two-point set = distance of values; determinate.

informal_statement: If s has a greatest element x and t has a least element y with x ≤ y, then the total variation of a function over the union s ∪ t equals the variation over s, plus the distance between f(x) and f(y), plus the variation over t.
gold_formal:
theorem union' (f : α → E) {s t : Set α} {x y : α} (hs : IsGreatest s x) (ht : IsLeast t y)
    (hxy : x ≤ y) :
    eVariationOn f (s ∪ t) = eVariationOn f s + edist (f x) (f y) + eVariationOn f t := sorry
gold_context: open scoped NNReal ENNReal Topology UniformConvergence
determinacy_reason: additivity of variation across a possibly-gapped join; determinate.

informal_statement: The total variation of the identity function over the part of a set in an interval [a,b] equals b − a.
gold_formal:
lemma eVariationOn_id {a b : ℝ} {s : Set ℝ} (as : a ∈ s) (bs : b ∈ s) :
    eVariationOn id (s ∩ Icc a b) = .ofReal (b - a) := sorry
gold_context: open scoped NNReal ENNReal Topology UniformConvergence
variable {f : α → ℝ} {s : Set α} {C : ℝ} {a b : α}
determinacy_reason: near-duplicate of the monotone-variation formula specialized to the identity; several sibling lemmas match.

informal_statement: The total variation of the identity function on a closed interval [a,b] equals b − a.
gold_formal: lemma eVariationOn_id_Icc (a b : ℝ) : eVariationOn id (Icc a b) = .ofReal (b - a) := sorry
gold_context: open scoped NNReal ENNReal Topology UniformConvergence

informal_statement: If a real-valued function has locally bounded variation on a set, then on that set it is the difference of two monotone (non-decreasing) functions; moreover the two monotone parts can be chosen so that their combined increments equal the total variation of the function.
gold_formal:
theorem LocallyBoundedVariationOn.exists_monotoneOn_sub_monotoneOn' {f : α → ℝ} {s : Set α}
    (h : LocallyBoundedVariationOn f s) :
    ∃ p q : α → ℝ, MonotoneOn p s ∧ MonotoneOn q s ∧ f = p - q ∧
      ∀ x ∈ s, ∀ y ∈ s, (p y - p x) + (q y - q x) = variationOnFromTo f s x y := sorry
determinacy_reason: Jordan decomposition with the variation-matching refinement; expressible informally.

informal_statement: A function between conditionally complete linear orders carrying their order topologies that is both left- and right-continuous in the order-theoretic sense (it preserves suprema of sets bounded below and infima of sets bounded above) is topologically continuous.
gold_formal:
lemma Continuous.of_ordContinuous (hl : LeftOrdContinuous f) (hr : RightOrdContinuous f) :
    Continuous f := sorry
gold_context: open Set Filter TopologicalSpace Topology Function
variable {α : Type u} {β : Type v} {γ : Type w}
determinacy_reason: order left+right continuity ⇒ topological continuity; expressible informally.

informal_statement: If a closed interval [a,b] (with a ≤ b) is contained in its own image under a continuous self-map f of an ordered space, then f has a fixed point in [a,b].
gold_formal:
theorem exists_mem_Icc_isFixedPt_of_surjOn {a b : α} {f : α → α} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b))
    (hle : a ≤ b) (h_surj : SurjOn f (Icc a b) (Icc a b)) : ∃ c ∈ Icc a b, IsFixedPt f c := sorry
gold_context: open Filter OrderDual TopologicalSpace Function Set
determinacy_reason: interval-covers-its-image ⇒ fixed point; hypotheses explicit.

informal_statement: If a continuous self-map maps an interval into itself, it has a fixed point in that interval.
gold_formal:
theorem exists_mem_uIcc_isFixedPt_of_mapsTo {a b : α} {f : α → α} (hf : ContinuousOn f (uIcc a b))
    (hmaps : MapsTo f (uIcc a b) (uIcc a b)) : ∃ c ∈ uIcc a b, IsFixedPt f c := sorry
gold_context: open Filter OrderDual TopologicalSpace Function Set
determinacy_reason: the informal statement does not distinguish the ordered-interval [a,b] from the unordered interval, nor 'maps-into' from 'covers', so multiple sibling lemmas match.

informal_statement: A monotone (order-preserving) function that is topologically continuous is left-order-continuous: it sends the supremum of any set that has one to the supremum of the images.
gold_formal:
theorem Continuous.leftOrdContinuous (cont : Continuous f) (hf : Monotone f) :
    LeftOrdContinuous f := sorry
gold_context: open Set Filter TopologicalSpace Topology Function
determinacy_reason: monotone + continuous ⇒ order-continuous; expressible, though 'left/right' convention is a modelling choice.
